name: Country Captain
recipeIngredient: 1 (5 1/2-to-6 lb) stewing hen or capon, cleaned and dressed
2 large Yellow onions; peeled and coarsely chopped
2 large Sweet green peppers; cored, seeded & coarsely chopped
1/4 cup Olive or other cooking oil
2 cans (1 lb, 12 oz) tomatoes, with their juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove all body fat from the cavity of the bird. Place the bird and giblets in a large kettle. Add bay leaves, celery, and water; cover and simmer 10-to-15 minutes. Remove the liver and reserve it.
2. Re-cover the chicken and simmer 1-to-1 1/2 hours or until tender. Lift the chicken from the kettle and cool. Strain the stock and reserve.
3. In the same kettle, stir-fry onions, green peppers, and garlic in oil over moderate heat for 8-to-10 minutes until onions are golden.
4. Add all remaining ingredients except almonds to the kettle; simmer, uncovered, 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Skin the chicken, remove meat from bones, and cut into bite-sized pieces. Dice giblets and liver. Add chicken, giblets, and liver to the kettle; simmer until heated through (15 minutes).
6. Remove bay leaves. Serve Country Captain over fluffy boiled rice and sprinkle each portion with toasted slivered almonds. Makes 6-8 servings
